title: Bisect, Reproducible Diagnosis, and Automated Regression Search · Conflict Triage
summary: A responder inherits a plausible but defective reproducible bisect harness and causal report; preserve reachable history, isolate the first causal Git-state mismatch, and retain a regression probe. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in reproducible bisect harness and causal report and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain binary search over candidate ancestry, good and bad polarity, convergence, and the assumptions that make a reported first bad commit meaningful.
Define known good and bad endpoints, path limits, terms, first-parent choices, and ancestry validity before the first trial.
Build a hermetic bisect run harness with explicit exit meanings, timeouts, cleanup, logs, deterministic fixtures, and no unsafe repository code execution.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Inspect the failure record
steps.instruction:
debug case — Case G20 is a debug in a disposable repository. the regression entered within the ancestry and path-limited candidate set. The learner must predict bisect selects a midpoint while preserving explicit good and bad polarity, run git bisect start bad-tip known-good -- src/parser.ts tests/parser.test.ts, explain the first trial halves 2400 candidates and records the tested object ID, test this changed condition: mark an unbuildable midpoint skip and report the remaining adjacent candidate range honestly, and preserve this recovery boundary: save git bisect log and reset to the original branch after retaining the regression probe.

Inspect the Gita Bisect Search Invariant trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for explain binary search over candidate ancestry, good and bad polarity, convergence, and the assumptions that make a reported first bad commit meaningful.

Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Bisect tests commits in timestamp order and always needs exactly log2(n) trials.

options.text: Inspect repository boundary, config origin, current operation, worktree, index, refs, and graph
Preserve uncommitted work and a recovery ref, then state intended tree and topology
Preview and perform the least destructive simulated Git operation
Verify status, staged and range diffs, graph, tests, remote target, and recovery path

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair
steps.instruction: Append an Advanced Git evidence block headed "# Evidence: gita-bisect-cause-verification-7ovnfc" with exact before and after refs; a falsifiable prediction; the bounded simulator command; expected, observed, tree, behavior, changed-case, and recovery evidence; and one honest real-repository transfer limit.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Gita Bisect Cause Verification into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: gita-bisect-cause-verification-7ovnfc
# scenario-key: trace-7ovnfc probe-cfnvo7 boundary-vnfc7o owner-7ovnfcx fault-7ovnfcz
# before-ref: main=a11ce7o candidate=b00b1fc
# hypothesis: the regression entered within the ancestry and path-limited candidate set
git bisect start bad-tip known-good -- src/parser.ts tests/parser.test.ts
# expected: bisect selects a midpoint while preserving explicit good and bad polarity
# observation: the first trial halves 2400 candidates and records the tested object ID
# changed-case: mark an unbuildable midpoint skip and report the remaining adjacent candidate range honestly
# after-ref: main=a11ce7o candidate=c0ffeec
# tree-proof: the affected tree and path set match the predicted repository transition
# behavior-proof: a changed input preserves the named product or release invariant
# recovery: save git bisect log and reset to the original branch after retaining the regression probe
# transfer-limit: execute real historical code only in an isolated environment with bounded credentials and network
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

steps.title: Publish the incident record
steps.instruction: Write the debug evidence note for Gita Bisect Cause Verification: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Case G28 is a debug in a disposable repository. the regression entered within the ancestry and path-limited candidate set. The learner must predict bisect selects a midpoint while preserving explicit good and bad polarity, run git bisect start bad-tip known-good -- src/parser.ts tests/parser.test.ts, explain the first trial halves 2400 candidates and records the tested object ID, test this changed condition: mark an unbuildable midpoint skip and report the remaining adjacent candidate range honestly, and preserve this recovery boundary: save git bisect log and reset to the original branch after retaining the regression probe.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Reproduce the boundary, inspect the candidate diff and dependencies, revert or patch experimentally, retain a regression test, and distinguish correlation from cause.
